CI=confidence interval; EOT = the average of week 22 and week 24 if no rescue therapy, otherwise EOT= last assessment prior to rescue therapy; IGF-1=insulin-like growth factor-1; IRT = Interactive Response Technology, LS= least squares; ULN= upper limit of normal.

Baseline IGF-1 is the average of the measurements taken on Day 1 on or prior to first dose of study drug and the last IGF-1 value measured just prior to this day. Least squares (LS) means, standard error, difference in least squares means and associated 95% confidence interval, and treatment p-value from an analysis of covariance with treatment group, prior treatment strata per IRT (medically naïve or previously treated versus washout) as fixed effects, baseline IGF-1xULN as a covariate.

Figure 14 shows the mean IGF-1×ULN (±SE) change from Baseline in IGF-1×ULN levels by visit in the RC Phase of the study for all participants without or prior to rescue at each visit by treatment group. IGF-1 values after rescue use were excluded.

In the paltusotine group, mean IGF-1 levels rapidly decreased by Week 2 and remained stable from Week 4 to Week 24, whereas in the placebo group, mean IGF-1 levels remained at Baseline levels through Week 8 and slightly decreased through Week 24, by which time 13 participants had received rescue therapy.

Figure 14 Mean (±SE) IGF-1xULN Change from Baseline by Visit in Randomized Controlled (RC) Phase of Study CRN00808-08 (Full Analysis Set) Participants Without/Prior to Rescue

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

<!-- image -->

IGF-1=insulin-like growth factor-1; SE= standard error; ULN= upper limit of normal. Pre-dose values are presented in the graph, except for Week 16 - post dose and Week 24 - anytime.

The  majority  of  participants  in  the  paltusotine  group  demonstrated  reductions  in  IGF-1  level  from Baseline that occurred rapidly between baseline and week 2 and continued, whereas those in the placebo group showed little change or increases from Baseline.

Approximately half of individual participants in the paltusotine group achieved IGF-1 levels ≤ 1.0 × ULN compared to only a few participants in the placebo group, which is consistent with the proportion of participants who achieved IGF-1 levels ≤ 1.0 × ULN for the primary endpoint.

EOT = the average of Week 22 and Week 24 if no rescue therapy, otherwise EOT = last assessment prior to rescue therapy; IGF-1=insulin-like growth factor-1; RC= Randomized Controlled; ULN= upper limit of normal.

## Other Secondary Efficacy Endpoints - RC Phase

## 1) Proportion of Participants with IGF-1 &lt;1.3×ULN at EOR

The  proportion  of  participants  who  achieved  IGF-1  level  &lt;1.3×ULN  was 66.7% (36  of  54)  in  the paltusotine group compared with 14.0% (8 of 57) in the placebo group. This difference was statistically significant ( p&lt;0.0001 ).

## 2) Proportion of Participants with GH&lt;1.0 ng/mL at Week 22

The proportion of participants who achieved GH level &lt;1.0 ng/mL at Week 22 (EOR) was 57.4% (31 of 54) in the paltusotine group compared with 17.5% (10 of 57) in the placebo group. This difference was statistically significant ( p&lt;0.0001 ).

| Rescue medication                | 0                        | 1 (6.7)                  | 1 (2.8)                  | 2 (3.7)                  | 13 (22.8)      |
| Dose up-titration after Week 12  | 0                        | 0                        | 1 (2.8)                  | 1 (1.9)                  | 0              |
| Discontinuation of treatment     | 2 (66.7)                 | 1 (6.7)                  | 1 (2.8)                  | 4 (7.4)                  | 16 (28.1)      |
| Odds ratio (Paltusotine/Placebo) |                          |                          |                          | 7.588                    |                |
| 95% CI for odds ratio            |                          |                          |                          | (2.776,                  |                |
|                                  |                          |                          |                          | 23.483)                  |                |
| P-value                          |                          |                          |                          | <0.0001                  |                |

EOR = end of Randomized Controlled Phase; GH=growth hormone; IRT = Interactive Response Technology. Average from the integrated GH sampling at week 22 is used to determine response. Percentages are calculated based on the number of participants in the Full Analysis Set. P-value, odds ratio, and corresponding 95% CI are from an exact logistic regression model with treatment group as a factor and a covariate for prior treatment strata per IRT (medically naïve or previously treated versus washout). For the odds ratio, placebo participants are the reference group.

## 3) Change from Baseline to EOR in Total ASD Score

The LS mean (±SE) changes from Baseline to EOT in total ASD score were -2.669 (±1.4221) for the paltusotine group and 2.754 (±1.3641) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -5.423 (95% CI: -9.070, -1.776) indicating an improvement and stabilization of symptoms for the paltusotine group, and worsening of symptoms for the placebo group. This difference was statistically significant ( p=0.0039 ) and clinically meaningful.

Table 26 Secondary Efficacy Endpoint: Change from Baseline to EOT in Total ASD Score in RC Phase of Study CRN00808-08 (Full Analysis Set)

|                                       |   Paltusotine (N=54) |   Placebo (N=57) | Treatment Difference (95% Confidence Interval)   |   P-value |
|---------------------------------------|----------------------|------------------|--------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| Total ASD Change from Baseline to EOT |                      |                  |                                                  |           |
| LS Mean                               |               -2.669 |            2.754 | -5.423 (-9.070,                                  |    0.0039 |

ASD=Acromegaly Symptoms Diary, EOT = week 24 if no rescue, otherwise EOT = last assessment prior to rescue, IRT = Interactive Response Technology, RC=randomized controlled.

Baseline total ASD is the sum of the weekly average on or prior to Day 1, and post-baseline total ASD is the sum of the weekly average on or prior to the scheduled visit date, inclusive of the date of visit, for 7 items (headache, joint pain, sweating, fatigue, weakness in legs, swelling, and numbness or tingling). Least squares (LS) means, standard error, difference in least squares means and associated 95% confidence interval, and treatment p-value from an analysis of covariance with treatment group, and prior treatment strata per IRT (medically naïve or previously treated versus washout), baseline total ASD score as a covariate.

Figure 16 presents the mean change from baseline in total ASD for participants without rescue or prior to rescue therapy at each visit by treatment group in the RC phase. ASD scores after rescue use were excluded.

For the paltusotine group, the mean total ASD score decreased by the first post-dose assessment at Week  2  and  generally  continued  to  decrease  through  Week  24  indicating  improvement  and/or stabilization of symptoms. For the placebo group, the mean total ASD remained near Baseline for all visits through Week 24.

ASD=Acromegaly Symptoms Diary, RC=Randomized Controlled, SE=standard error Note: Baseline total ASD is the sum of the weekly average on or prior to Day 1, and post-baseline total ASD is the sum of the weekly average on or prior to the scheduled visit date, inclusive of the date of visit, for 7 items (headache, joint pain, sweating, fatigue, weakness in legs, swelling, and numbness or tingling).

When evaluating the ASD by individual item scores, all 7 items trended in favour of paltusotine, 5 of which were nominally significant (headache pain, sweating, fatigue, weakness in legs, swelling; nominal p&lt;0.05).  The  overall  total  ASD  score  was  not  driven  by  a  single  item,  indicating  that  there  were improvement  and  stabilization  across  all  acromegaly  symptoms  evaluated  in  the  paltusotine  group compared with the placebo group.

The  LS  means  (±SE)  change  from  Baseline  to  EOT  in  most  bothersome  symptoms  were -0.553 (±0.4219)  in  the  paltusotine  group  and 0.357 (±0.3770)  in  the  placebo  group,  with  a  treatment difference of -0.910 (95% CI: -1.976, 0.157) in favour of paltusotine (nominal p=0.0935 ).

## Main exploratory endpoints- RC phase

## 1) Change from Baseline to Week 22 in GH Level

The LS means (±SE) change from Baseline to EOT ( Week 22 if no rescue , otherwise EOT was the last assessment prior to rescue) in GH level (ng/mL) were -2.686 (±1.9109) for the paltusotine group and 1.636 (±1.2193) for  the placebo  group,  with  a  treatment  difference of -4.322 (95% CI: -8.537, 0.0106).

## 2) Proportion of Participants Who Received Rescue Therapy

The proportion of participants who received rescue therapy was 3.7% (2 of 54) in the paltusotine group and 22.8% (13 of 57) in the placebo group affirming the treatment effect of paltusotine versus placebo.

## 3) Time from Randomization to Initial Response (IGF-1 ≤ 1.0 × ULN)

The  median  (IQR)  time  to  initial  response  was 44  days (15.0  days,  not  estimable  [NE])  for  the paltusotine group and was not reached (NE, NE) for the placebo group.

## 4) Change from Baseline in Residual Tumour Volume at EOR

Pituitary tumour volume was evaluated by pituitary MRI and assessed by the Investigator for clinically significant safety findings and by an MRI central radiologist for tumor volume measurement/assessment.

At  EOT,  a  clinically  significant  tumour  volume  reduction  (as  measured by  &gt;20%  reduction  in tumor volume) was observed in 4 ( 11.8% ) participants in the Paltusotine group and in 0 (0.0%) participants in the placebo group. Four placebo participants had ≥ 20% increase in tumor volume compared to zero (0) Paltusotine participants. Paltusotine was associated with stable or reduced pituitary tumor size as measured by MRI.

## 4) Treatment Preference Questionnaire at EOR

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

In the paltusotine group ( N=54 ), once daily oral medication was preferred by 42 participants ( 77.8 %), previous injections were preferred by 5 participants ( 9.3% ), 5 participants (9.3%) had no preference, and data were missing for 2 participants (3.7%).

In the placebo group ( N=57 ), oral medication was preferred by 36 participants ( 63.2% ), previous injections were preferred by 6 participants ( 10.5% ), 9 participants ( 15.8% ) had no preference, and data were missing for 6 participants ( 10.5% ).

## 5) Summary of AcroQoL, EQ-5D-5L, PGI-S, PGI-I

- The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the EQ-5D-5L health index score were 0.001 (±0.0251)  for  the  paltusotine  group  and -0.102 (±0.0256)  for  the  placebo  group,  with  a treatment difference of 0.103 (95% CI: 0.038, 0.168) (nominal p=0.0023 ).

The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the EQ VAS score were -0.120 (±2.2489) for the paltusotine group and -5.573 (±2.3049) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of 5.453 (95% CI: -0.413, 11.319) (nominal p=0.0681 ).

- -PGI-I at EOR (EOT at Week 24): the LS means (±SE) for the PGI-I at EOT were -1.488 (±0.1792) for the paltusotine group and -1.040 (±0.1964) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -1.040 (95% CI: -1.536, -0.544) (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ).
- -Change from Baseline to EOR (EOT at Week 24) in the PGI-S : The LS means (±SE) change from Baseline to EOT in PGI-S were 0.030 (±0.1078) for the paltusotine group and 0.181 (±0.1004) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -0.151 (95% CI: -0.424, 0.122) (nominal p=0.275 ).
-  Change  from  Baseline  to  EOR  (EOT  at  Week  24)  in  AcroQoL  composite  scores  and standardized scores: The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the AcroQoL total score using composite scores were -0.506 (±2.0731) for the paltusotine group and -4.327 (±2.0543) for  the  placebo  group,  with  a  treatment  difference  of 3.821 (95%  CI:  -1.639,  9.281)  (nominal p=0.1676 ).

The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the AcroQoL total score using standardized scores were -0.575 (±2.3558) for the paltusotine group and -4.917 (±2.3344) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of 4.343 (95% CI: -1.862, 10.547) (nominal p=0.1676 ).

## Main exploratory endpoints-OLE Phase

Eligible participants who directly entered the OLE following screening initiated the study at Week 24 for the OLE Phase. OLE Baseline was defined as the last non-missing assessment prior to the first dose of study drug in OLE Phase.

## 1) Proportion of Participants with IGF-1 ≤ 1.0 × ULN and &lt;1.3 × ULN at each Visit in the OLE Phase

Table 28 presents the proportion of participants with IGF1 level ≤1.0×ULN and IGF -1 level ≤1.3×ULN by visit in the OLE Phase of Study CRN00808-08. The starting dose of paltusotine in the OLE phase was 20 mg QD, and most participants who were receiving 40 mg QD or 60 mg QD at the end of the RC phase were anticipated to up-titrate to 40 mg QD or 60 mg QD during the OLE phase.

The proportion of participants who achieved IGF1 level ≤1.0×ULN ranged from 51.3% to 100% from Week 36 to Week 144 for the overall population ( 51.0% to 100% for the prior paltusotine group and 37.0% to 100% for the prior placebo group) and ranged from 50.0% to 80.0% for the directly enrolled group from Week 36 to Week 120.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

The proportion of participants who achieved IGF-1 level &lt;1.3×ULN ranged from 69.9% to 100% from Week 36 to Week 144 for the overall population ( 68.6% to 100% for the prior paltusotine group and 65.4% to 100% for the prior placebo group) and was 100% from Week 36 to Week 120 for the directly enrolled group.

The starting paltusotine dose for the OLE was 20 mg resulting in a temporary down titration for prior paltusotine  participants  from  final  dose  in  the  RC  Phase  for  most  participants and  a  subsequent increase  in  IGF-1  levels  at  Week  26 .  Most  participants  who  were  responders  in  the  RC  phase continued to be responders in the OLE phase.

## · Medically naïve patients

Table 29 presents the proportion of participants with IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN and IGF -1 ≤1.3×ULN by visit in the OLE phase for the medically naïve group. Results are presented based on the final paltusotine dose in  the  RC  phase.  Three  medically  naïve  participants  in  the  prior  paltusotine  group  were  receiving paltusotine 40 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase; at Week 96, all 3 participants had IGF-1 ≤1.0×ULN at Week 96 during the OLE phase. Eighteen participants in the prior paltusotine group received paltusotine 60 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase. Four of these participants had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN at Week 60 and Week 108.

The proportion of medically naïve patients with IGF1  levels  ≤  ULN  increased  over  time  from 25% (11/44) at W36 to 35.7% (10/28) at W84. Likewise, the proportion of participants wih IGF-1 &lt; 1.3x ULN from 45.5% (20/44) at week 36 to 75% (21/28) at W84.

During the OLE phase, after starting at paltusotine 20 mg QD at Week 24, the majority of participants in the prior paltusotine group ( 81.0%, 17 of 21) and the prior placebo group ( 61.1%, 11 of 18) up- titrated  to  paltusotine  60  mg  QD  by  Week  48.  The  majority  of  participants  continued  to  receive paltusotine 60 mg QD at subsequent timepoints in the OLE phase. Of note, participants in the medically naïve group had higher baseline IGF-1 levels compared to the previously treated and washout groups. These patients may require a longer treatment duration to achieve biochemical control.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

## · Previously treated patients

During the OLE phase, after starting at paltusotine 20 mg QD at Week 24, the majority of participants ( 66.7%, 10 of 15) in the prior paltusotine group up-titrated to paltusotine 40 mg QD by Week 36. The majority of participants continued to receive paltusotine 40 mg QD at subsequent timepoints in the OLE Phase. One participant was on paltusotine 20 mg QD at Week 96.

In the prior placebo group, after starting at paltusotine 20 mg QD at Week 24, the majority of participants ( 58.8%, 10 of 17) up-titrated to 60 mg QD by Week 60. The majority of participants in the prior placebo group continued to receive paltusotine 60 mg QD at subsequent timepoints in the OLE Phase. Two participants  in  the  prior  placebo  group  were  receiving  paltusotine  20  mg  QD  at  Week  108  and  1 participant at Week 120.

Table 30 presents the proportion of participants with IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN and IGF -1 ≤1.3×ULN by visit in the OLE phase for the previously treated group. Results are presented based on the final paltusotine dose in the RC phase. Seven participants in the prior paltusotine group who were receiving paltusotine 40 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN at Week 84, and those who continued to be followed up to Week 120 had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN. Nine participants in the prior paltusotine group received paltusotine 60 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase. At Week 60, 5 of 7 (71.4%) of participants who received paltusotine 60 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN, with  the  majority  of  participants  who  were  followed  at  subsequent  weeks  achieving  IGF-1  level ≤1.0×ULN.

In the OLE phase, the proportion of previously treated participants with IGF1 levels ≤ ULN increased over  time  from 55.9% (19/34)  at  W36  to 73.1% (19/26)  at  W84.  Likewise,  the  proportion  of participants with IGF-1 levels &lt; 1.3xULN from 73.5% (25/34) at W36 to 96.2% (25/26) at W84.

## · Washout patients

Table 31 presents the proportion of participants with IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN and IGF -1 ≤1.3×ULN by visit in the OLE phase for the washout group. Results are presented based on the final paltusotine dose in the RC phase. Four participants in the prior paltusotine group who were receiving paltusotine 40 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN through Week 84, and those who continued to be followed up to Week 96 had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN. Nine participants in the prior paltusotine group received paltusotine 60 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase. At Week 60, 5 of 8 (62.5%) of participants who received paltusotine 60 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN, with the majority of participants who were followed at subsequent visits achieving IGF1 level ≤1.0×ULN. One participant who received 20 mg QD as their final dose in the RC phase had IGF1 ≤1.0×ULN at Week

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

96. Among the 36 washout participants, the majority of participants (84.6%, 11 of 13) in the prior paltusotine group up titrated to paltusotine 40 mg QD by Week 36. The majority of participants in the prior paltusotine group continued to receive paltusotine 40 mg QD at subsequent timepoints in the OLE phase. In the prior placebo group, after starting at paltusotine 20 mg QD at Week 24, the majority of participants (90.9%, 10 of 11) up-titrated to 40 mg QD by Week 36. The majority of participants in the prior placebo group continued to receive paltusotine 40 mg QD at subsequent timepoints in the OLE phase.

In the OLE phase, the proportion of the washout participants with IGF1 levels ≤ULN increased over time from 80% (28/35) at W36 to 85.3% (29/34) at W84. Likewise, the proportion of participants with IGF1 levels &lt; 1.3xULN increased from 97.1% (34/35) at W36 to 100% (34/34) at W84.

The  applicant  provided  the  proportion  of  patients  who  used  adjunctive  therapies  (cabergoline, bromocriptine) from W48 in the OLE phase of study CRN00808-08 in each subgroup as recommended in the protocol.

As of the data snapshot date of 21 May 2025 and also at the data snapshot date of 31 August 2025 (as no additional participant need adjunctive therapies from 21 May 2025), the need for adjunctive therapies in study CRN00808-08 was very limited, with 18 of 114 ( 15.8% ) participants. The use of adjunctive therapies  was  observed  more  frequently  in  participants  receiving  prior  placebo  ( 23.1%; 12  of  52 participants)  than  in  participants  receiving  prior  paltusotine  ( 11.8%; 6  of  51  participants)  and  no participants  in  the  directly  enrolled  group.  Overall,  12  participants  (10.5%)  received  cabergoline,  6 participants (5.3%) received bromocriptine mesylate, and 1 participant received bromocriptine (0.9%).

When the 18 participants who received adjunctive therapies in study CRN00808-08 were excluded from the response analyses, the proportion of participants without adjunctive therapy who had IGF1 ≤ 1.0 x ULN were similar to the proportion of participants with IGF1 ≤ 1.0 x ULN in the overall group ( 62.0% without adjunctive vs 54.7% overall at Week 60; 74.3% without adjunctive vs 63.6% overall at Week 72; 73.8% without adjunctive vs 65.4% overall at Week 84). Similar trends continued up through Week 120. The proportion of participants with IGF1 ≤ 1.3 x ULN were also similar between the 2 groups at these visits.

## 2) Change from OLE Baseline in IGF-1×ULN to the Visits in the OLE Phase

Figure 18 presents mean IGF-1 levels (×ULN) by visit for participants in the RC phase and OLE phase of study  CRN00808-08.  Continued  maintenance  and  durability  of  mean  IGF-1  levels  were  observed  in participants in the prior paltusotine group up to Week 144. From W36 to W144, there was a constant decrease of the IGF-1 levels with a negative change from baseline at each visit highlighting a reduction of the IGF-1 levels over time.

In the prior placebo and directly enrolled groups, decreases in mean IGF-1 levels were observed over the initial 2 weeks with paltusotine in the OLE phase, which were consistent with the onset and magnitude of reduction in IGF-1 levels in the paltusotine group in the RC phase, and were maintained throughout the OLE phase.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

After the dose titration period of the OLE phase, the results show that despite having high baseline IGF1 levels, participants in the medically naïve group showed clinically meaningful reductions in IGF-1 levels, and with a longer treatment duration, these participants were able to achieve meaningful treatment benefits with paltusotine as evidenced by the further decreases in IGF-1 levels during the OLE phase.

## 3) Change from OLE Baseline in Total ASD Score to Visits in the OLE Phase

Figure  22  presents  the  mean  ASD  Total  Score  by  visit  for  participants  in  the  OLE  phase  of  study CRN00808-08. Mean ASD Total Scores generally remained stable throughout the OLE phase. In the OLE phase, ASD scores were collected only once at quarterly study visits. Because the ASD was designed for daily administration, sparse data collections would be expected to result in some degree of visit-to-visit variability, reflecting sporadic variations over the course of a single day prior to the data collection date.

Figure 22 Mean (±SE) ASD Total Score by Visit in OLE Phase of Study CRN00808 08 (OLE Safety Analysis Set)

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

ASD=Acromegaly Symptoms Diary; OLE=Open-label Extension; RC=Randomized Controlled; SE=standard error Note: OLE Baseline is Week 24.

<!-- image -->

Data cutoff date: 31 August 2025

A post hoc analysis of the RC phase data from study CRN00808-08 was performed to evaluate the mean change  from  baseline  at  the  end  of  treatment  in  ASD  scores  for  the  most  bothersome  symptoms (headache, joint pain, sweating, fatigue, weakness in legs, swelling, numbness or tingling, difficulty sleeping, and difficulty with short-term memory), as reported by participants across the three subgroups - medically naïve, previously treated, and washout participants.

An analysis of covariance (ANCOVA) model was used and all analyses were performed using the Full Analysis Set (FAS). Among participants who were medically naïve, the least squares (LS) mean changes in  ASD scores for the most bothersome symptoms from baseline to the end of treatment showed a numerical improvement with paltusotine compared to placebo ( 0.206 [n=57] with paltusotine versus +0.284  with  placebo  [n=54]),  resulting  in  a  between  treatment  group  difference  of  -0.490  (95% confidence interval [CI]: -2.161 to 1.180) . The findings were similar among the previously treated and washout subgroups, with numerical improvements observed in participants who received paltusotine compared with those who received placebo

In  summary,  this  analysis  demonstrates  that  ASD  scores  for  the  most  bothersome  symptoms  were improved  in  the  medically  naïve  population  in  line  with  the  other  participants  with  uncontrolled acromegaly,  regardless  of  clinical  status  and  baseline  characteristics  but  the  difference  was  not significant for any subgroup of patients.

Figure 23 presents mean GH levels (ng/mL) by visit for participants in the OLE phase of study CRN0080808.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

Continued maintenance and durability of mean GH levels (ng/mL) were observed from OLE Baseline up to Week 144 in participants in the prior paltusotine group and up to Week 120 in the directly enrolled group.

Participants in the prior placebo group showed a decrease in mean GH level by Week 26 that remained stable through Week 144.

GH=growth hormone; OLE=Open-label Extension; RC=Randomized Controlled; SE=standard error; ULN=upper limit of normal

Note: OLE Baseline is Week 24. GH level at OLE Baseline was based on the average of fasting integrated GH measurements at the Week 22 visit for participants enrolled from the RC Phase and the average of all measurements taken from fasting integrated GH at the screening visit for participants enrolled directly in OLE Phase. All subsequent GH measurements in OLE Phase were based on single random samples at the visit. Data cutoff date: 31 August 2025

## 5) Change from OLE Baseline in Residual Tumor Volume at Each Visit in the OLE Phase

No clinically significant changes (&gt;25% increases or decreases in tumour volume) were observed in pituitary tumour size in 20 participants at Week 48 as measured by MRI for either prior paltusotine group or prior placebo group.

## Pre-defined subgroup analyses

Subgroup analyses were conducted on the FAS for the primary endpoint for the following subgroups: sex, age, screening IGF-1 group, prior treatment (strata), BMI groups, weight groups, ethnicity, race, use of H2 blockers, and geographic region. Age was dichotomized to less than 65 years old and greater than or equal to 65 years old. Race was broken down to White and non-White.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

For the subgroups of unknown ethnicity, use of H2-blockers, and medically naïve participants, the 95% CI for the treatment difference included zero. However, the sample size for these subgroups was small, and the treatment differences were greater than zero and directionally favoured paltusotine.

## Pre-defined sensitivity analyses

## 1) Sensitivity Analyses of the primary endpoint

To assess the robustness of the primary endpoint analysis, the following prespecified sensitivity analyses were performed on the primary efficacy endpoint. Results of these sensitivity analyses are summarized for the paltusotine and placebo groups in Table 33.

In Stratum 1 (medically naïve or previously treated), the proportion of patients with IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN was 42.5% (17 of 40) in the paltusotine group compared with 2.4% (1 of 42) in the placebo group.

In Stratum 2 (washout), the proportion of patients with IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN was 92.9% (13 of 14) in the paltusotine group compared with 13.3% (2 of 15) in the placebo group. Within Stratum 1, the proportion  of  patients  with  IGF-1  level ≤ 1.0 × ULN  was 22.7% (5  of  22)  in  the  paltusotine  group compared with 4.2% (1 of 24) in the placebo group among medically naïve participants and was 66.7% (12 of 18) in the Paltusotine group compared with 0% (0 of 18) in the placebo group among previously treated participants.

P-value, odds ratio, and corresponding 95% CI are from an exact logistic regression model with treatment group as a factor. For the odds ratio, placebo participants are the reference group.

## -Medically naïve patients

46 patients were medically naïve:

Among the 22 medically naïve patients who were treated with paltusotine:

-  there  were 1/22 (4.5%) patient  treated  with  paltusotine  20  mg , 3/22 (13.6%) patients treated with paltusotine 40 mg and 18/22 (81.8%) patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg .
- only 5/22 ( 22.7% ) patients were responders: 0 responder at dose of paltusotine 20 mg, 3/5 ( 60%) responders at the dose of paltusotine 40 mg and 2/5 ( 40%) responders at the dose of paltusotine 60 mg. (3/3) 100% of  the  patients  treated with paltusotine 40 mg and 2/18 ( 11.1% )  of  the  patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg were responders.

In the 24 medically naïve patients who were treated with placebo: only 1/24 ( 4.2% ) patient was responder.

## - Patients previously treated

36 patients previously treated with other SRLs:

Among the 18 patients previously treated with other SRLs were treated with paltusotine:

- there were 1/18 (5.6%) patient treated with paltusotine 20 mg, 8/18 (44.5%) patients treated with paltusotine 40 mg and 9/18 (50%) patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg.
- -12/18 patients (66.7%) were responders: no patient treated with paltusotine 20 mg was responder, 6/12 ( 50% ) patients treated with paltusotine 40 mg and 6/12 ( 50% ) patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg. 6/8 ( 75% ) of the patients treated with paltusotine 40 mg and 6/9 ( 66.7% ) of the patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg were responders.

In the 18 patients treated with placebo, 0 were responders.

- 1 patient treated with paltusotine 40 mg and 1 patient treated with paltusotine 60 mg received rescue therapy and 4 patients in the group of placebo.
- 1 patient treated with paltusotine 20 mg, 1 patient treated with paltusotine 40 mg, 1 patient treated with paltusotine 60 mg and 4 patients on placebo have discontinued the treatment.

## - washout patients

There were 29 washout patients

Among the 14 washout patients who were treated with paltusotine:

- There were 1 patient treated with paltusotine 20 mg, 4 patients treated with paltusotine 40 mg, and 9 patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg.
- 13/14 were responders ( 92.9% ): 1/13 ( 7.7% ) patient treated with paltusotine 20 mg, 4/13 ( 30.7% ) patients treated with paltusotine 40 mg, and 8/13 ( 6.15% ) patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

were responders. 1/1 (100%) of the patients treated with paltusotine 20 mg, 4/4 of the patients treated with paltusotine 40 mg and 8/9 (88.9%) of the patients treated with paltusotine 60 mg were responders.

In the 15 washout patients who were treated with placebo, 2 were responders ( 13.3% ).

No patient received rescue therapy in the group of patients treated with paltusotine versus 3 patients in the group on placebo.

6 patients have discontinued the treatment in the group on placebo.

## 2) Sensitivity analysis was performed using a multiple imputation approach

For the key secondary endpoint of change from Baseline to EOR in IGF-1 x ULN, a sensitivity analysis was performed using a multiple imputation approach for missing data. In this analysis, missing IGF-1 results and all assessments following rescue therapy were imputed by multiple imputation using ontreatment intermediate values from the placebo group, which took into consideration the variability from natural disease course prior to medical intervention.

For this sensitivity analysis, the LS mean (±SE) changes from Baseline to EOT in IGF-1×ULN were -0.827 (±0.0823) for the paltusotine group and 0.124 (±0.0855) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -0.950 (95% CI: -1.166, -0.735) (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ). This result was consistent with the primary analysis of change from Baseline to EOR in IGF-1xULN.

## 3) Sensitivity Analyses of Key Secondary Endpoint by Stratum

- -In  Stratum  1 ,  the  LS  mean  (±SE)  changes  from  Baseline  to  EOT  in  IGF-1×ULN  were -0.887 (±0.0903) for the paltusotine group and 0.070 (±0.0881) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -0.957 (95% CI: -1.210, -0.705) (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ).
- -In Stratum 2 , the LS mean (±SE) change from Baseline to EOT in IGF-1×ULN were -0.600 (±0.1044) for the paltusotine group and 0.152 (±0.1008) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -0.752 (95% CI: -1.052, -0.453) (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ).

## Ancillary analyses

## 1) Group 2 Post Hoc Analyses

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

Post hoc analyses were conducted to further characterize Group 2 (previously treated) participants in study  CRN00808-08,  including  supplemental  data  collection  of  historical  data  and  a  retrospective reclassification and efficacy analyses based on disease control on previous treatment.

A retrospective re-classification based on detailed historical data allowed for a more informative grouping of  participants  who  were medically naïve or not successfully treated (Stratum 1) or  Stratum 2 with recently washed out or had an extended washout following a response. Stratum 1 was reconfigured inclusive of participants who were medically naïve, previously uncontrolled, and with status unknown relative to disease control.

Figure 24 Post-hoc  Re-analysis  of  Group  2  Participants  Based  on  Prior  History  of Biochemical Control

## Original Analysis

## Group 2 Re-Analysis

<!-- image -->

A post-hoc analysis  of  the  primary  efficacy  endpoint  (IGF-1 ≤ 1 × ULN at the End of the Randomized Control Phase), and all secondary endpoints were performed after conducting this re-classification of Group 2 (previously treated) based on the presence or absence of disease control on previous acromegaly treatment(s).

## IGF-1 Response in Participants Who Received Any Prior Medical Therapy

Prior response data from the 36 participants enrolled in Group 2 ('previously treated') who received any previous treatment, 14 had presence of disease control and 13 had absence of disease control while on previous acromegaly medication(s). In 9 participants , the response to previous medical therapy was unknown, either because IGF-1 was not performed or because the results were not available and these participants were classified as medically naïve.

Results from the post-hoc analysis with the re-classified Groups 1 and 2 in Stratum 1 and Expanded Group 3 in Stratum 2 are summarized in Table 38.

## 2)  A  post-hoc  analysis  was  performed  to  evaluate  the  data  for  participants  without  prior surgery in study CRN00808-08

Study participants without prior pituitary surgery were slightly older than the overall population (median age 59.5 years versus 46.0 years ). Participants without prior surgery had a longer median time since acromegaly diagnosis compared to the overall study population (105.3 months versus 72.2 months; CSR CRN00808-08 RC Phase.

No other notable differences were observed in Baseline demographic characteristics between participants without  prior  pituitary  surgery  and  the  overall  study  population.  Baseline  disease  characteristics, including IGF-1 levels and time since acromegaly diagnosis, were also generally consistent between participants without prior pituitary surgery and the overall population. Of note, all the medically naïve patients had prior pituitary surgery.

Figure 25 presents the mean IGF-1 levels (×ULN) by visit for participants without prior pituitary surgery in the RC Phase and OLE Phase of the study.

## 5.3.2.2. Study CRN00808-09

## 5.3.2.2.1. Study title

## PATHFNDR-1: Paltusotine Acromegaly Therapy Featuring a Non-Invasive Daily Regimen

## 5.3.2.2.2. Study design

This is a phase 3, randomized, placebo-controlled, multicentre study designed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of paltusotine in participants with acromegaly who were switched from monotherapy with injected long-acting octreotide or lanreotide. In the Randomized Controlled (RC) Phase, eligible participants were randomized (1:1 ratio) to daily oral administration of paltusotine or placebo when their next dose of octreotide/lanreotide was due to begin, for a Treatment Period of up to 36 weeks .

Participants who completed the RC phase could be eligible to enrol and receive paltusotine in the Openlabel Extension (OLE) Phase for up to 200 weeks . The RC phase is completed. This report describes results from RC Phase. The OLE phase is on-going.

The  study  design  was  aimed  at  maintaining  participants  in  their  baseline  biochemical  status  and optimizing their dose while minimizing the period in which their IGF-1 level was above Baseline.

This  study  was  conducted  at  30  sites  in  13  countries:  Argentina,  Belgium,  Brazil,  Bulgaria,  France, Hungary, Israel, Italy, Peru, Poland, Serbia, United Kingdom (UK), and United States (US).

Figure 26 CRN00808-09 Study schema

Study Screening (≤12weeks)

<!-- image -->

Average IGF-1=average of all (up to 3) study assessments taken during Screening, EOR=end of RC Phase (end of treatment), EOS=End of Study, IGF-1=insulin-like growth factor-1, LA SRL=long-acting somatostatin receptor ligand (octreotide/lanreotide only), OLE=Open-label Extension, RC=Randomized Controlled, ULN=upper limit of normal

EOR was defined as the end of the RC Phase at Week 36.

EOS is defined as the day final study data are collected and should occur 4 weeks after the last dose of study drug.

The end of Week 240/EOS Visit (Follow-up Visit for the OLE Phase) is the last planned visit of the study, but the EOS Visit could occur at Week 40 (Follow-up Visit for the RC Phase) in participants who did not enter the OLE Phase.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

The study consisted of 3 periods:

- -Screening Period : the Screening Period consisted of 2 to 3 visits over 4 to 12 weeks . The last prerandomization long-acting SRL (octreotide/lanreotide) injection was to be administered before or during Screening to maintain the dosing interval between octreotide/lanreotide and the first dose of study drug, ie, the first dose of study drug was to be administered when the next dose of lanreotide/octreotide would have  been  due,  provided  the  screening  period  did  not  exceed  12  weeks.  As  such,  the  last octreotide/lanreotide injection was the key temporal event during screening.
- -Treatment Period (Randomized Controlled Phase):

Participants meeting all eligibility criteria were randomized (1:1) to paltusotine or placebo during the RC Phase, which lasted approximately 36 weeks. Randomization was stratified by mean Screening IGF-1 level (&lt;0.86 × ULN, ≥ 0.86 × ULN) and prior treatment (lanreotide, octreotide). Scheduled study visits occurred approximately every 4 weeks.

- -Open-label Extension (OLE) Phase (cut-off date: 21 May 2025)

Participants who completed the 36-week RC Phase or who met rescue criteria and completed the 36week RC Phase with rescue treatment could continue participation in the OLE Phase if the participant was willing  to  participate  and,  in  the  opinion  of  the  investigator,  the  participant  could  benefit  from continued or initiated treatment with paltusotine.

## Treatment

## RC Phase

The starting dose was 40 mg (2×20 mg tablets of active paltusotine or matching placebo) QD for oral self-administration. During the RC Phase, the dose of study drug could be increased from 40 mg (2×20 mg tablets) QD to 60 mg (3×20 mg tablets) QD through Week 24 if the participant's most recent IGF1  result  was  &gt;0.9×ULN  and  tolerability  at  the  current  dose  was  acceptable,  as  evaluated  by  the investigator.

It was anticipated that the study drug dose would be stable, and titration would be completed, prior to or at Week 24 , with no dose titrations/adjustments after Week 24 . Participants who required dose up-titration after Week 24 were considered non-responders.

Dose down-titration  was  allowed  at  any  time  during  the  study  if  the  dose  was  not  tolerable  in  the judgement of the investigator.

If tolerability improved following the dose reduction, the dose could be increased in 20 mg increments up to a maximum dose of 60 mg. If study drug was not tolerated, treatment could be interrupted for a total of 14 days per year, but no more than 7 consecutive days per year during the study, followed by resumption of study drug at the same or at a reduced dose, as appropriate

Rescue  criteria  were  provided  for  participants  who  required  resumption  of  standard  acromegaly treatment during the RC Phase.

Table 40 Dose Titration Options for Study CRN00808-09

| Dose Titration/Adjustment   | IGF-1 Criteria                                               | Tolerability Criteria                                             |
|-----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 40 mg → 60 mg               | Participant's most recent IGF-1 result on 40 mg was >0.9×ULN | Increase allowed due to acceptable tolerability on 40 mg          |
| 40 mg → 20 mg               | --                                                           | Dose reduction required due to unacceptable tolerability on 40 mg |
| 20 mg → 40 mg               | Participant's most recent IGF-1 result on 20 mg was >0.9×ULN | Tolerability was acceptable on 20 mg                              |

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

IGF-1=insulin-like growth factor-1, ULN=upper limit of normal

| Dose Titration/Adjustment   | IGF-1 Criteria   | Tolerability Criteria                                             |
|-----------------------------|------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 60 mg → 40 mg               | --               | Dose reduction required due to unacceptable tolerability on 60 mg |

## OLE Phase

The starting dose for participants in the OLE phase was paltusotine 40 mg QD , dispensed in an openlabel manner. The first dose of the OLE phase was administered at the Week 36/EOR Visit . Participants were given bottles of paltusotine containing 36×20 mg tablets and were instructed to take 2 tablets daily until their next visit.

Dose escalation in the OLE phase could occur once the first IGF-1 result was available. At that time, the investigator could determine whether the dose could be up-titrated to 60 mg . Scheduled visits for the OLE phase occur approximately every 12 weeks. The OLE treatment period is 204 weeks.

Dose down-titration was allowed if the dose was not tolerable in the judgement of the investigator. Study medication could be held for up to a total of 14 days, but no more than 7 consecutive days per year in the OLE.

## Concomitant medications

## Prohibited Medications

- Oral estrogen, except for monophasic estrogen-progestin oral contraception
- Strong inducers of the drug metabolizing enzyme CYP3A4, including but not limited to: apalutamide, carbamazepine,  enzalutamide,  mitotane,  phenytoin,  rifampin,  and  St.  John's  wort  (because  these medicines could decrease the concentration of paltusotine in the systemic circulation).
- Any standard acromegaly drug that was not used as a rescue medication during the RC Phase or an allowed adjunctive medication in the OLE Phase
- Any other investigational drug, unless approved by the Medical Monitor (e.g. coronavirus disease 2019 [COVID-19] therapies)

## Rescue therapy

## RC Phase

-  During  the  RC  Phase,  acromegaly  treatments  other  than  study  drug  were  prohibited,  unless  the participant met criteria for rescue therapy. Octreotide LAR or lanreotide depot monotherapy (approved SRLs) were the only rescue therapies permitted during the study.

Criteria for rescue therapy consisted of the following:

Significant  worsening  of  1  or  more  acromegaly  symptoms or  the  development  of  a  new acromegaly  symptom, at  the  highest  dose  (60  mg)  for  at  least  2  weeks ,  as  assessed  by  the investigator.  Symptoms  needed  to  be  attributed  to  uncontrolled  acromegaly  in  the  opinion  of  the investigator  and  could  include  but  were  not  limited  to  the  following:  headache,  arthralgia,  fatigue, hyperhidrosis, or soft tissue swelling.

Significant worsening was defined as symptoms requiring a substantial increase in level of clinical care (e.g. significant intervention needed to avert hospitalization or a clinically notable increase in frequency or intensity of patient contact required) or substantial clinical deterioration (e.g. worsening from a mild to severe AE or the onset of an SAE would meet these criteria).

## AND

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

IGF-1 value ≥ 1.3 × ULN at the highest dose (60 mg) measured at 2 successive planned visits or at an unscheduled visit if an earlier result was needed (as evaluated by the investigator).

If rescue therapy was required during the RC phase, paltusotine or placebo were to be discontinued before rescue therapy was initiated. Study participants receiving rescue therapy were asked to continue study visits for surveillance for the remainder of the 36-week RC phase.

Participants who discontinued from study drug early by meeting protocol criteria for rescue therapy with approved SRLs and who completed the 36-week RC phase could be eligible for participation in the OLE Phase of the study, if the participant was willing and, in the opinion of the investigator, the participant could benefit from continued or initiated paltusotine treatment.

## OLE Phase

At 6 months, if there was evidence of paltusotine efficacy but participants had not reached therapeutic target goals, adjunctive treatment could be initiated if clinically appropriate beginning at Week 60 . If  these criteria were met, non-SRL acromegaly treatments, such as cabergoline or the GH receptor antagonist  pegvisomant,  were  permitted  and  could  be  initiated  as  an  adjunctive  treatment  when  a participant experienced at least 2 consecutive elevated IGF-1 ( ≥ 1.3 × ULN) measurements at the highest paltusotine  dose  level  (60  mg)  or  was  otherwise  not  at  the  participant's  therapeutic  target  in  the judgement of the investigator.

Short-  and  long-acting  somatostatin  receptor  agonists  (other  than  paltusotine) remained prohibited therapies during the OLE Phase. If these treatments were needed, participants were to be discontinued from the study, and an ET Visit was to be scheduled prior to resuming these treatments.

## Randomisation

Participants were randomized to study treatment using interactive response technology (IRT). Randomization was performed using a fixed-block randomization scheme. The randomization scheme was generated prior to the initiation of the study by an independent statistician/programmer. Investigators  and  the  study  team  were  not  aware  of  the  block  size  of  the  randomization  scheme.

Randomization  was  stratified  by  IGF-1  level (&lt;0.86 × ULN, ≥ 0.86 × ULN) and  prior  treatment (lanreotide or octreotide).

## Blinding

The Sponsor, contract research organizations (CROs), investigators, study site personnel, and study participants were blinded to treatment assignment during the RC phase. The randomization schedule was kept strictly confidential and accessible only to authorized persons. The randomization schedule was made  available  for  unblinding  and  analysis  only  after  the  RC  phase  was  completed,  the  protocol deviations were determined, and the study database was locked.

The IRT system was programmed with blind-breaking instructions in case of an emergency for participant safety and for expedited reporting of SAEs to regulatory agencies, when required.

## Patient population

## Main inclusion criteria

(also see other similar inclusion criteria in study CRN00808-08 )

- Adults ≥ 18 years of age with medically stable, confirmed-active acromegaly and on an approved, stable monotherapy dose of long-acting octreotide or lanreotide, for at least 12 weeks prior to Screening. Continuous treatment with octreotide or lanreotide must be at least 24 weeks prior to Screening.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

The following prior treatment regimens were allowed for inclusion into this study: long-acting octreotide: 10, 20, 30, 40 mg every 4 weeks or long-acting lanreotide: 60, 90, 120 mg every 4 weeks or 120 mg every 6 or 8 weeks.

- Previous diagnosis of acromegaly confirmed by the Investigator and approved by the Medical Monitor. This  requires  evaluable  documentation  of  a  pituitary  tumour  diagnosed  by  pituitary  imaging  or histopathologic confirmation of a pituitary adenoma at least 24 weeks prior to screening.
- For subjects who have had pituitary surgery, there must be documentation of IGF-1 concentration ≥ 1.3 × ULN at least 12 weeks after last pituitary surgery. The surgery must have been performed ≥ 24 weeks prior to screening. Subjects who have not had pituitary surgery must have documentation of IGF1 concentration ≥ 1.3 × ULN performed ≥ 24 weeks prior to screening.
- -Screening average IGF-1 levels of ≤ 1.0 × ULN . The upper limit for eligibility is mean IGF-1 of 1.04 (rounded to 2 decimal places) and will be based on 2 or 3 separate measurements in consultation with the Medical Monitor.
- Willing and able to undergo biliary/gallbladder ultrasound procedure and pituitary MRI during screening and during the study.

## Main exclusion criteria

Similar exclusion criteria to study CRN00808-08. In addition, treatment-naïve or treatment-withdrawn acromegaly subjects should be excluded.

- -Treatment-naïve or treatment-withdrawn acromegaly subjects.
- Use of the following medications as outlined:
- .  Pasireotide LAR (within 24 weeks prior to screening),
- . Pegvisomant (within 12 weeks before screening),
- . Dopamine agonists (within 12 weeks before screening), or
- . Short acting somatostatin analogues (SA-SSAs) within last 12 weeks before the first dose of study drug.

Note:  Withdrawal  of  these  medications  should  be  part  of  the  subject's  medical  care  plan  prior  to screening; entry into the study should not be the sole reason for withdrawal of a prior medication.

## 5.3.2.2.3. Objectives and estimands

## Primary objective

The primary objective of the RC phase was to evaluate the efficacy of paltusotine versus placebo on IGF-1 response in participants with acromegaly treated with long-acting SRLs.

## Estimands for the primary objective

Table 42 Estimands for primary objective

| Population                                      | Patients with acromegaly (previously treated with somatostatin receptor ligands) who would encounter the intercurrent event of discontinuation, rescue medication, prohibited medication, or dose up-titration after Week 24 if assigned to either paltusotine or placebo.   |
|-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Treatment conditions                            | Assignment to paltusotine, regardless of discontinuation and use of rescue medication, compared to assignment to placebo, regardless of discontinuation and use of rescue medication.                                                                                        |
| Endpoint (variable)                             | Proportion of participants maintaining IGF- 1 ≤ 1.0×ULN at end of randomised treatment (EOR), based on the average of Weeks 34 and 36. If only one value is available, that single result (rounded to two significant figures) is used.                                      |
| Population-level summary                        | Proportion of responders in each treatment group; comparison via odds ratio using exact logistic regression with adjustment for baseline IGF-1 group and prior treatment (lanreotide or octreotide). If the model fails to converge, Firth logistic regression is applied.   |
| Intercurrent events and strategy to handle them | Intercurrent events and strategy to handle them                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| Discontinuation of treatment                    | Composite: to reflect treatment failure                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Rescue medication use                           | Composite: to reflect treatment failure                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Use of prohibited medication                    | Composite: to reflect treatment failure                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Up-titration after Week 24                      | Composite: to reflect treatment failure                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

The primary estimand assesses the effect of paltusotine versus placebo on maintenance of biochemical control in acromegaly, defined as sustaining IGF1 ≤ 1.0×ULN at Week 36 (based on the average of Weeks 34 and 36, or a single value if only one is available). Participants who discontinued treatment, received rescue or prohibited medication, or had dose up-titration after Week 24 were considered nonresponders. These intercurrent events were handled using a composite strategy, reflecting clinical failure.

## Statistical methods for estimation and sensitivity analysis on primary estimands

The primary efficacy analysis was based on the Full Analysis Set (FAS). The estimand targeted the proportion of participants with IGF-1 ≤ 1.0 × ULN at End of Randomisation (EOR), defined as the average of Weeks 34 and 36 (rounded to two significant figures). Participants with missing IGF-1 values at both visits  or  with  intercurrent  events  (treatment  discontinuation,  rescue/prohibited  medications,  or  dose titration after Week 24) were considered non-responders.

The primary analysis used an exact logistic regression model adjusting for baseline IGF-1 level and prior treatment  strata;  Firth  logistic  regression  was  prespecified  as  a  fallback.  Multiplicity  was  controlled through  a  hierarchical  testing  procedure.  Sensitivity  analyses  explored  variations  in  the  estimand, including per-protocol, completers, and alternate handling of intercurrent events (e.g. up-titration not considered treatment failure).

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

## Secondary objectives

Secondary objectives were to evaluate the effects of paltusotine versus placebo on IGF-1 level, GH response, and acromegaly symptoms.

The objective of the OLE phase is to evaluate the long-term safety and efficacy of paltusotine.

## Secondary Efficacy Endpoints

The secondary efficacy endpoints of the RC Phase were as follows:

- Change from Baseline in IGF-1, in units of ULN, to EOR
- Proportion of participants with GH&lt;1.0 ng/mL at Week 34, out of those who had GH &lt;1.0 ng/mL at Baseline
- Change from Baseline in total Acromegaly Symptom Diary (ASD) score to EOR.

| Population                                      | Patients with acromegaly previously treated with lanreotide or octreotide who would encounter the intercurrent event of discontinuation, rescue medication, prohibited medication, or dose up- titration after Week 24 if assigned to either paltusotine or placebo.                                                            |
|-------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Treatment condition<s>                          | Assignment to paltusotine, regardless of discontinuation and use of rescue medication, compared to assignment to placebo, regardless of discontinuation and use of rescue medication.                                                                                                                                           |
| Endpoint (variable)                             | Change from baseline to end of randomised treatment (Week 36) in IGF-1×ULN. EOR is defined as the average of IGF-1 values at Weeks 34 and 36. If only one value is available, that value is used. If neither is available, the last value prior to rescue, prohibited medication use, discontinuation, or up-titration is used. |
| Population-level summary                        | Treatment group difference in adjusted means based on ANCOVA model, including baseline IGF-1×ULN, screening IGF-1 strata (<0.86×ULN / ≥0.86×ULN), and prior treatment (lanreotide, octreotide) as fixed effects. Least squares means, standard errors, 95% confidence intervals and p-values are reported.                      |
| Intercurrent events and strategy to handle them | Intercurrent events and strategy to handle them                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |

## Statistical methods for estimation and sensitivity analysis on the secondary estimands

The key secondary efficacy analysis was based on the Full Analysis Set (FAS) and evaluated change from baseline  in  IGF-1×ULN  at  End  of  Randomisation  (EOR).  The  analysis  used  an  ANCOVA  model  with treatment group, baseline IGF-1×ULN strata, and prior treatment group (lanreotide, octreotide) as fixed effects, and baseline IGF-1×ULN as a covariate.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

Missing data following intercurrent events (rescue/prohibited medication, up-titration after Week 24, or dropout) were imputed using the last available pre-event value. A sensitivity analysis was conducted using multiple imputation, applying on-treatment intermediate values from the placebo group to reflect the natural course of the disease.

## Exploratory objectives

The exploratory efficacy endpoints for the RC phase were as follows:

- Change from baseline to Week 34 in GH
-  Proportion of participants who received rescue therapy
-  Proportion of participants who achieved IGF-1&lt;1.3×ULN at EOR
-  Proportion of participants with GH&lt;2.5 ng/mL at Week 34
-  Change from baseline in residual tumor volume at EOR
-  Time from randomization to the first IGF-1&gt;1.0×ULN for 2 consecutive visits
-  Time from randomization to the first IGF-1 ≥ 1.3×ULN for 2 consecutive visits
- Change from baseline to EOR in Acromegaly Quality of Life (AcroQoL) total score
-  Treatment preference questionnaire (TPQ) at EOR
- Change from Baseline to EOR in EuroQol-5 Dimensions 5-Level (EQ-5D-5L) health index, EuroQol Visual Analogue Scale (EQ VAS), and dimension scores

## 5.3.2.2.4. Results

## Participant flow and numbers analysed

Data cut-off date: 5 December 2023

Interim analysis: 21 May 2025

Interim analysis: 31 October 2025

## RC phase

A  total  of 115  participants were  screened,  of  which 58  participants  were  randomized ( 30 participants  in  the  total  paltusotine  group  and  28  participants  in  the  placebo  group )  and included in the FAS) for the RC Phase of the study.

Treatment groups were balanced with respect to randomization stratification factors (Screening IGF-1 groups [&lt;0.86×ULN, ≥ 0.86 × ULN] and prior treatment [lanreotide, octreotide]).

## Figure 27 Participant Disposition for RC Phase of Study CRN00808-09

OLE=Open-label Extension, RC=Randomized Controlled, RC=randomized controlled, TEAE=treatment-emergent adverse event

## Treatment Discontinuation

In the paltusotine group, one treatment discontinuation due to an adverse event and one met the rscue criteria.

In the placebo group, 17 patients discontinued the treatment as they met the rescue criteria.

## Rescue therapy

In the paltusotine group, 3 participants received octreotide or lanreotide as rescue therapy during the RC Phase of the study.

A total of 17 participants ( 60.7%) in the placebo group received octreotide or lanreotide as rescue therapy during the RC Phase.

## OLE Phase

Of the 58 participants randomized in this study, 53 participants ( 91.4% ) entered the OLE Phase. All of these participants received at least 1 dose of paltusotine and were included in the OLE Safety Analysis Set.

Of these 53 total participants, 27 participants were from the paltusotine group, and 26 participants were from the placebo  group in  the  RC  phase  of  the  study.  One  participant  discontinued  study treatment and the study due to the inability to fulfil study requirements and procedures.

At the cut-off date of August/September 2024, 2 participants (both in the prior placebo group) had dose reductions from 40 mg to 20 mg due to a TEAEs. For both participants, the events were reported as resolved and disease control was maintained.

Page 2 of 2

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

At the data snapshot date of 31 August 2025, eight participants (15.1%) overall discontinued the OLE phase of the study : three participants in the prior paltusotine group and five participants in the prior placebo group.

| Protocol Version 2.0, dated 24 February 2021 (participants enrolled in study beginning with Protocol Version 2.0) | Removed Group 2 (IGF- 1 level >1.0×ULN and ≤1.5×ULN) from the study population after discussion with Health Authority.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Protocol Version 3.0, dated 14 September 2021                                                                     | Primary endpoint analysis was revised based on the average of last 2 measurements from Week 34 and Week 36 (EOR) instead of the last 3 measurements from Week 32, Week 34, and Week 36 after discussion with Health Authority. After discussion with Health Authority, clarified that it was anticipated that the study drug dose would be stable, and titration completed prior to or at Week 24, with no dose titrations/adjustments after Week 24. Clarified that up-titration after Week 24 should not occur; participants who required dose up-titration after Week 24 would be considered nonresponders. After discussion with Health Authority, clarified that participants who were rescued during the RC Phase of the study should remain in the study and complete the study assessments. Clarified that these participants could be eligible for enrollment into the OLE Phase of the study. Addition of exploratory endpoints for loss or response (time from randomization to first IGF-1 >1×ULN for 2 consecutive visits, time from randomization to first IGF- 1 ≥1.3×ULN for 2 consecutive visits). Change in residual tumor volume changed from a safety endpoint to an exploratory efficacy endpoint at request of regulatory authorities. Addition of monthly pregnancy testing throughout the study. |

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

| Protocol Version or Clarification Letter                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Summary of Key Changes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Protocol Clarification Letter 1 to Protocol Version 2.0 and Version 3.0, dated 09 December 2021 (Clarification letter applied to both Protocol Versions 2.0 and 3.0 because Protocol Version 3.0 was still under review in some countries.) | In SOA footnote, clarified that the screening fasting integrated GH at this timepoint was during Screening, and there was no study drug dosing during the Screening Period. The collection timeframe of the ASD was updated in Protocol Version 3.0 to 2 weeks prior to study drug dosing. This update was made in '1.2 Schedule of Activities: Randomized, Controlled Phase'; however, the corresponding body text was inadvertently not updated to match. Exclusion Criterion #23 (renal function) clarified to exclude creatinine level, as exclusion based on renal function already provided as Exclusion Criterion #18. Correction of PGI-I and PGI-S description in body of protocol (no change to scale used in protocol). Clarification to procedures for unblinding. The date and reason for breaking the blind was to be recorded in the source documentation but not recorded in the eCRF for the study. Inclusion criteria #5 (Screening average IGF- 1 levels of ≤1.0×ULN) and average IGF-1 level for IGF-1 response revised to clarify only one rounding procedure to determine eligibility, ie, to 2 decimal places. |
| Protocol Clarification Letter 2 to Protocol Version 3.0, dated 29 November 2022                                                                                                                                                             | Clarified timing of pituitary MRI and biliary/gallbladder ultrasound in SOA and body of protocol. Clarified objective of OLE Phase (to evaluate long-term safety and efficacy of paltusotine).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Protocol Clarification Letter 3 to Protocol Version 3.0, dated 05 April 2023                                                                                                                                                                | Clarification that gallbladder ultrasounds could be performed at any time during the study if clinically indicated in the opinion of the Investigator.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Protocol Version 4.0, dated 21 November 2023                                                                                                                                                                                                | Extended the OLE Treatment Period by 2 years (ie, from 96 weeks to 200 weeks). Implemented ophthalmic monitoring in response to a Health Authority request to collect additional data to facilitate evaluation of the clinical relevance of a phototoxicity study in rodents. Ophthalmic assessments added as a safety endpoint to OLE Phase. Harmonized protocol to align with protocol clarification letters as described above.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |

## Protocol deviations

## RC phase

Important protocol deviations (PDs) were reported for 8 participants ( 13.8% ) in the FAS; all of which pertained to ASD questionnaires not being completed during screening. No major protocol deviations were reported. One participant in the placebo group had a protocol deviation due to COVID-19. The majority of PDs were administrative, and none of the PDs were deemed to have a significant impact on study efficacy or safety.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

## OLE Phase

No important or major protocol deviations were reported during the OLE phase. The majority of protocol deviations were administrative, and none of the protocol deviations were deemed to have a significant impact on study safety or efficacy.

## Baseline data

Table 46 presents a summary of demographic and other baseline characteristics for the FAS.

Most demographic and other baseline characteristics were consistent between treatment groups. The median age for all participants was 55.0 years ,  ranging from 29 to 84 years, higher percentage of participants overall were receiving octreotide compared with lanreotide (58.6% versus 41.4%) as their long-acting SRL therapy prior to study enrolment, with most participants receiving mid and high doses of both SRLs (mid doses: lanreotide 90 mg/month or 120 mg/6 weeks or octreotide 20 mg/month; high doses: lanreotide 120 mg/month or octreotide 30 mg/month).

A higher percentage of participants in the paltusotine group were male compared with the placebo group ( 50.0% versus 39.3% ), and the paltusotine group had a higher mean weight ( 83.7 kg versus 73.2 kg) and BMI ( 29.9 kg/m2 versus 27.6 kg/m2) compared with the placebo group. A higher percentage of participants in the paltusotine group had screening HbA1c level ≥ 6.5% compared with the placebo group ( 23.3% versus 7.1% ).

Percentages are calculated based on the number of participants in the Full Analysis Set. SRL = somatostatin receptor ligand. Screening IGF-1 value is defined as the average of the IGF-1 values from all the Screening Visits.

## Disease Characteristics

Most  disease  characteristics  were  consistent  between  the  paltusotine  and  placebo  groups,  including Baseline mean (SD) IGF-1×ULN levels ( 0.828 [0.144] and 0.822 [0.159], respectively) and Baseline mean (SD) GH (ng/mL) levels ( 0.916 [1.023] and 0.886 [0.831], respectively).

Study  participants  overall  had  long-standing  disease  prior  to  enrolment,  with  a  higher  mean  (SD) duration since acromegaly diagnosis in the paltusotine group compared with the placebo group ( 186.6 [88.3] months versus 120.8 [81.9] months).

The  majority  of  participants  overall  ( 86.2% )  had  prior  pituitary  surgery,  with  a  higher  mean  (SD) duration since pituitary surgery in the paltusotine group compared with the placebo group ( 171.8 [89.4] months versus 102.4 [65.0] months).

Baseline GH is defined as the average of all the measurements taken from fasting integrated GH samples at Screening Visit.

## Extent of Exposure

RC Phase

Of the 30 participants in the paltusotine group, 14 participants (46.7%) received paltusotine 40 mg QD and 16 participants (53.3%) received paltusotine 60 mg QD as their final dose . No participants received 20 mg as their final dose in the RC phase.

Only 1 participant in the paltusotine 40 mg group had a dose reduction to 20 mg due to TEAEs , which were constipation, diarrhoea, soft faeces, flatulence, nausea, and fatigue.

## OLE phase

At the data snapshot date of 31 August 2025, the median duration (range) of exposure to paltusotine in the OLE phase was 126.0 weeks (9.4 to 175.0 weeks) for the total population (127.1 weeks [24.3 to 175.0 weeks] for the prior paltusotine group and 124.9 weeks [9.4 to 158.7 weeks] for the prior placebo group.

At the snapshot date of 21 May 2025 , during the OLE phase, after starting at paltusotine 40 mg QD at Week 36, 61.5% (16 of 26) participants in the prior paltusotine group remained on paltusotine 40 mg QD by Week 60 and 38.5% (10 of 26) escalated to 60 mg QD. Approximately one-half of participants continued to receive paltusotine 40 mg QD at subsequent timepoints in the OLE Phase, while the other one-half of participants escalated to 60 mg QD.

In the prior placebo group, after starting at 40 mg QD at Week 36, approximately 70% to 80% of participants continued to receive paltusotine 40 mg QD at subsequent timepoints in the OLE phase, while the remaining 20% escalated to 60 mg QD.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

## Outcomes and estimation

## 1) Primary Efficacy Endpoint - RC Phase

The proportion of participants who maintained IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN was 83.3% (25  of  30)  in  the paltusotine  group  compared  with 3.6% (1  of  28)  in  the  placebo  group.  This  study  met  statistical significance on the primary endpoint ( p&lt;0.0001 ) favouring paltusotine over placebo.

At  EOR, 14  patients were  treated  with  paltusotine  40  mg,  and 16  patients were  treated  with paltusotine 60 mg. Among the 25/30 patients who maintained IGF1 levels ≤ ULN, 14/25 ( 56% ) in the paltusotine 40 mg group and 11/16 ( 44% ) in the paltusotine 60 mg group.

Of note, there was 5/16 ( 31.3% ) of patients treated with 60 mg who are non-responders versus 0% of patients treated with 40 mg.
