Percentages are calculated based on the number of participants in the Full Analysis Set. ULN is determined based on the participant's age and sex.

P-value, odds ratio, and corresponding 95% CI are from an exact logistic regression adjusting for Screening IGF-1 groups (&lt;0.86xULN, &gt;=0.86xULN), and prior treatment with Lanreotide or Octreotide. For the odds ratio, placebo participants are the reference group.

## Secondary Efficacy Endpoints

## 1) Change from Baseline to EOR in IGF-1×ULN

Table 50 presents results for the secondary efficacy endpoint of change from Baseline to EOR in IGF1×ULN, where EOR was EOT at Week 36 if there was no rescue therapy administered or was based on the last available IGF-1 assessment prior to rescue. The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOT in IGF-1×ULN were 0.042 (±0.093) for the paltusotine group and 0.833 (±0.096) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -0.791 (95% CI: -1.057 to -0.525). This difference was statistically significant ( p&lt;0.0001 ).

Least squares (LS) means, standard error, difference in least squares means and associated 95% confidence interval, and treatment p-value from an analysis of covariance with treatment group, Screening IGF-1xULN groups (&lt;0.86xULN, &gt;=0.86xULN) and prior treatment group with lanreotide or octreotide as fixed effects, Baseline IGF1xULN as a covariate.

Figure 28 shows the mean change from Baseline in IGF-1 level (×ULN) for participants who did not receive rescue therapy at each visit by treatment group. IGF-1 values after rescue therapy use were excluded.

In the paltusotine group, mean IGF-1 levels were maintained at all scheduled visits from Baseline to Week 36, whereas in the placebo group, mean IGF-1 levels were increased at Week 4 through Week 36, by which time 17 participants in the placebo group had received rescue therapy.

## 2) Proportion of Participants with GH&lt;1.0 ng/mL at Week 34

A total of 23 participants in the paltusotine group and 18 participants in the placebo group had GH level &lt;1.0 ng/mL at Baseline. The proportion of participants who maintained GH level &lt;1.0 ng/mL at Week 34 was 87.0% (20 of 23) in the paltusotine group compared with 27.8% (5 of 18) in the placebo group. This study met statistical significance on this endpoint ( p=0.0003 ) favouring paltusotine over placebo.

## 3) Change from Baseline to EOR in Total ASD Score

The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOT in total ASD score were -0.606 (±1.504) for the paltusotine group and 4.558 (±1.593) for the placebo group, indicating stabilization of symptoms for the paltusotine group and significant worsening of symptoms for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -5.164 (95% CI: -9.536 to -0.792). This study met statistical significance ( p=0.0216 ) on this endpoint favouring paltusotine over placebo.

## Main exploratory Efficacy Endpoints - RC Phase

## 1) Proportion of Participants Who Received Rescue Therapy

The proportion of participants who received rescue therapy was 6.7% ( 2 of 30 ) in the paltusotine group and 60.7% ( 17 of 28 ) in the placebo group (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ) affirming the treatment effect of paltusotine versus placebo.

## 2) Proportion of Participants Who Achieved IGF-1 &lt;1.3×ULN at EOR

The proportion of participants who maintained IGF-1 level &lt;1.3×ULN was 93.3% (28  of  30)  in  the paltusotine group compared with 28.6% (8 of 28) in the placebo group (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ).

## 3) Change from Baseline to EOR in Residual Tumour Volume

No clinically significant decreases or increases (as measured by &gt;25% reduction or increase in tumour volume) were observed in the change from Baseline to EOR in residual tumour volume.

## 4) Time from Randomization to First IGF-1&gt;1.0×ULN for 2 Consecutive Visits

Participants needed to be on a dose of 60 mg QD (paltusotine or placebo) for at least 2 weeks in order to be considered as having an event.

The Kaplan-Meier analyses were performed using 2 different methods:

- 1) participants who discontinued treatment prior to having the event were counted as having the event at the time of treatment discontinuation: the median (IQR) time to loss of biochemical control was not reached (IQR: 141.0 days, not estimable [NE]) for the Paltusotine group and was 83.0 days (IQR: 57.0, 85.0) for the placebo group.
- 2) participants who discontinued treatment prior to reaching IGF-1&gt;1.0×ULN were censored at the time of the last assessment prior to discontinuation of treatment: The median (IQR) time to loss of biochemical control was not reached (IQR: 141.0 days, NE) for the Paltusotine group and was 84.0 days (IQR: 58.0, 85.0) for the placebo group.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

## 5) Treatment Preference Questionnaire at EOR

In the paltusotine group (N=30), oral medication was preferred by 18 participants ( 60.0% ), previous injections were preferred by 6 participants ( 20.0%), 4 participants ( 13.3%) had no preference, and data were missing for 2 participants ( 6.7%). In the placebo group (N=28), oral medication was preferred by 17 participants ( 60.7% ), previous injections were preferred by 5 participants ( 17.9% ), 3 participants ( 10.7% ) had no preference, and data were missing for 3 participants ( 10.7% ).

## 6) Summary for AcroQoL, EQ-5D-5L, PGI-S, PGI-I

- Acro-QoL: The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the AcroQoL total score using composite scores were 0.268 (±2.480) for the paltusotine group and 3.557 (±3.676) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -3.289 (95% CI: -13.220 to 6.643) (nominal p=0.4844 ). The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the AcroQoL total score using standardized scores were 0.304 (±2.818)  for  the  paltusotine  group  and 4.041 (±4.178)  for  the  placebo  group,  with  a treatment difference of -3.737 (95% CI: -15.023 to 7.548) (nominal p=0.4844 ).

-EQ-5D-5L health index, EQ VAS: The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the EQ-5D-5L health index score were -0.152 (±0.037) for the paltusotine group and -0.037 (±0.051) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -0.114 (95% CI: 0.255 to 0.026) (nominal p=0.0951 ).

The LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOR in the EQ VAS score were -11.073 (±4.230) for the Paltusotine group and 2.498 (±6.048) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -13.571 (95% CI: -30.132 to 2.989) (nominal p=0.0938 ).

-PGI-I at EOR (EOT at week 36): The LS means (±SE) for the PGI-I at EOT were -0.933 (±0.272) for the paltusotine group and -0.058 (±0.438) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of 0.875 (95% CI: -1.922 to 0.172) (nominal p=0.0984 ).

## Main exploratory Efficacy Endpoints - OLE Phase

Although the sample sizes for subsequent timepoints (e.g. Weeks 156 to 204) were small, results are also presented in the source tables for subsequent timepoints per the scheduled efficacy assessments specified.

## 1) Proportion of Participants with IGF-1 ≤ 1.0 × ULN and &lt; 1.3 × ULN at Each Visit

The starting dose of paltusotine in the OLE phase was 40 mg QD day, and most participants who were receiving 60 mg/day at the end of the RC phase were anticipated to up-titrate to 60 mg/day during the OLE phase.

The proportion of participants who achieved IGF1 level ≤1.0×ULN ranged from 74.5% to 93.3% from Week 48 to Week 180 for the overall population ( 50.0% to 96.0% for the prior paltusotine group and 77.3% to 100% for  the prior placebo group). (Note: Only 1 participant reached the timepoints for Week 192 and Week 204 at the time of the data snapshot.)

The proportion of participants who achieved IGF-1 level &lt;1.3×ULN ranged from 95.7% to 100% from Week 48 to Week 180 for the overall population ( 92.0% to 100% for the prior paltusotine group and 92.9% to 100% for the prior placebo group).

## 2) Change from OLE Baseline in IGF-1×ULN to the Visits in the OLE phase

A total of 53 participants (27 in the prior paltusotine group and 26 in the prior placebo group) had IGF1 results at OLE Baseline.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

At the data snapshot date of 31 August 2025, continued maintenance and durability of mean IGF-1 levels ≤1.0×ULN were observed in participants in the prior paltusotine group up to Week 180, while a decrease in mean IGF-1 level was observed over the initial 2 weeks in the prior placebo group that was maintained to Week 180. (Note: Only 1 participant reached the timepoints for Week 192 and Week 204 at the time of the data snapshot.)

## 3) Proportion of Participants with GH &lt;1.0 ng/mL at Each Visit in the OLE Phase Out of Those Who had GH &lt;1.0 ng/mL at OLE Baseline

At the cut-off date of 5 December 2023, at Week 60, GH results were available for 37 participants ( 20 in the prior paltusotine group and 17 participants in the prior placebo group ) who had GH&lt;1.0 ng/mL at OLE Baseline.

The proportion of participants who maintained GH level &lt;1.0 ng/mL at Week 60 was 81.1% (30 of 37) for the total population ( 80.0% [16 of 20] for the prior paltusotine group and 82.4% [14 of 17] for the prior placebo group).

## 4) Change from OLE Baseline in Total ASD Score to Visits in the OLE Phase and mean ASD total score by visit in the OLE Phase

At the data cut-off of 5 December 2023, a total of 41 participants (20 in the prior paltusotine group and 21 in the prior placebo group) had ASD score results at OLE Baseline and Week 60.

The mean change (±SE) in the total ASD score from OLE Baseline to Week 39 was -0.027 (±0.8273) for the total population ( 1.004 [±0.1.1492] for the prior paltusotine group and -1.403 [±1.1203] for the prior placebo group showing a decrease for the total population and the prior placebo group at Week 39.

The mean change (±SE) in the total ASD score from OLE Baseline to Week 60 was 2.642 (±1.1253) for the total population ( 3.219 [±1.4986] for the prior paltusotine group and 2.092 [±1.6977] for the prior placebo group).

At  the  data  snapshot  date  of  31  August  2025,  mean  ASD  Total  Scores  generally  remained  stable throughout the OLE phase. In the OLE phase, ASD scores were collected only at quarterly study visits. Because the ASD was designed for daily administration, sparse data collections would be expected to result in some degree of visit-to-visit variability, reflecting sporadic variations over the course of a single day prior to the data collection date.

## 5) Change from OLE Baseline in GH to the Visits in the OLE Phase and mean GH levels (ng/mL) by visit for participants in the OLE Phase of Study CRN00808-09

At the data cut-off date of 5 December 2023, a total of 52 participants ( 27 participants in the prior paltusotine group and 25 participants in the prior placebo group) had GH results reported at OLE Baseline and Week 60 .

For these participants, the mean (±SE) GH level (ng/mL) at OLE Baseline was 0.937 (±0.1398) for the total population ( 0.938 [±0.2348] for the prior paltusotine group and 0.936 [±0.1480] for the prior placebo group).

The mean change (±SE) in GH (n/mL) from OLE Baseline to Week 60 was 0.420 (±0.1686) for the total population ( 0.357 [±0.1887] for the prior paltusotine group and 0.488 [±0.2891] for the prior placebo group) demonstrating a stable result for all participants that was maintained through Week 84 .

Although the sample sizes after Week 60 were small, similar results were observed at Weeks 72, 84, 96, and Week 120.

At the data snapshot date of 31 August 2025, mean GH levels in the OLE phase showed long-term maintenance and durability from OLE Baseline through Week 180.

## 6) Change from OLE Baseline in Residual Tumour Volume to the Visits in the OLE Phase

At the data cut-off of 5 December 2023, a summary of the change in residual pituitary volume from OLE Baseline  to Week  84 was  provided  for 11  participants .  No  clinically  significant  changes  (&gt;25% increases or decreases in tumour volume) were observed in pituitary tumour size as measured by MRI.

## 7) Proportion of Participants who Received Adjunctive Therapy in the OLE Phase

As  of  the  data  cut-off  date  of  5  December  2023,  no  participants  received  permitted  adjunctive acromegaly treatment during the OLE Phase.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

## 8) Summary of AcroQoL, EQ-5D-5L, PGI-S, PGI-I

At the data cut-off of 5 December 2023,

-  ACRO-QoL  and  EQ -5D-5L  health  index,  EQ  VAS,  and  dimension  scores: A  total  of 14 participants (6 in the prior paltusotine group and 8 in the prior placebo group) had results reported for AcroQoL total scores at OLE Baseline and Week 84 .

No significant changes from OLE baseline were highlighted.

-PGI-S: total of 43 participants (20 in the prior paltusotine group and 23 in the prior placebo group) had PGI-S results at OLE Baseline and Week 60.

No significant changes from OLE baseline were highlighted.

## Pre-defined subgroup analyses

Subgroup analyses were conducted on the FAS for the primary endpoint for the following subgroups: sex, age group, screening IGF-1 group, prior treatment, BMI groups, weight groups, race, ethnicity, use of PPI and H2 blockers, and geographic region.

No notable subgroup effects were observed for any subgroup, showing that the paltusotine treatment effect was consistent across subgroups. For the subgroups of unknown ethnicity and non-White and unknown race, the 95% CI for the treatment difference included zero. However, the sample size for these  subgroups  was  small,  and  the  treatment  differences  were  greater  than  zero  and  directionally favoured paltusotine.

## Pre-defined sensitivity analyses

## 1) Sensitivity Analyses of the primary endpoint

To assess the robustness of the primary endpoint analysis, the following prespecified sensitivity analyses were  performed  on  the  primary  efficacy  endpoint:  1)  completers  analysis,  which  included  only  FAS participants who completed at least 34 weeks of treatment, 2) where participants were not considered non-responders for up-titrating in dose after Week 24, and 3) on the PPS. The analyses were conducted using the same methodology for the primary endpoint.

For  the  completers  analysis, 28 participants in  the  paltusotine  group  and 11 participants in  the placebo  group  completed  34  weeks  of  randomized  treatment.  The  proportion  of  participants  who maintained IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN was 89.3% (25 of 28) in the paltusotine group compared with 9.1% (1 of 11) in the placebo group (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ). These results were consistent with the results of the primary endpoint.

No participants up-titrated in dose after Week 24, so results of this sensitivity analysis were identical to the results of the primary endpoint analysis.

The PPS included 57 participants (30 participants in the paltusotine group and 27 participants in the placebo group). One participant in the placebo group was excluded from the PPS for not completing the RC phase of the study. In the PPS, the proportion of participants who maintained IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN was 83.3% (25 of 30) in the paltusotine group compared with 3.7% (1 of 27) in the placebo group (nominal p&lt;0.0001 ).

## 2) Sensitivity analysis using a multiple imputation approach

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

A  sensitivity  analysis  was  performed  using  a  multiple  imputation  approach  for  missing  data  for  the secondary efficacy endpoint of change from Baseline to EOR in IGF-1×ULN. In this analysis, IGF-1 results for all assessments following the first occurrence of intercurrent events, such as dropouts, taking rescue therapy or prohibited medications, or up titration in dose after Week 36, were evaluated as missing data.

The  missing  measurement  was  imputed  by  a  multiple  imputation  procedure  using  on-treatment intermediate values from the placebo group, which took into consideration the variability from the natural disease course prior to medical intervention.

The imputation models included treatment group and stratification factors as fixed effects, along with by-visit IFG-1×ULN values as covariates.

However, there were a substantial amount of placebo participants (17 out of 28 participants) that went on rescue therapy. To overcome model convergence, treatment group, stratification factors, and baseline IGF-1×ULN values were excluded from the imputation model(s).

For this sensitivity analysis, the LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to Week 36 in IGF-1×ULN were 0.045 (±0.108)  for  the  paltusotine  group  and 0.767 (±0.316)  for  the  placebo  group,  with  a treatment difference of -0.722 (95% CI: -1.373 to -0.071; nominal p=0.0301 ).

Results of this sensitivity analysis were consistent with results of the primary analysis of the secondary efficacy endpoint.

In addition, missing data on IGF-1×ULN were imputed using a multiple imputation procedure that utilized all observed data from both treatment groups to overcome the convergence issue.

For this analysis, the LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to Week 36 in IGF-1×ULN were 0.041 (±0.089) for the paltusotine group and 0.783 (±0.120) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -0.742 (95% CI: -1.032 to -0.453; nominal p&lt;0.0001 ).

## Ancillary analyses

A post hoc analysis was performed to evaluate the data for participants without prior surgery.

In the RC phase of Study CRN00808-09 , 8 participants (4  in  the paltusotine group and 4 in the placebo group) had not undergone prior pituitary surgery.

Baseline demographic and disease characteristics were generally consistent between participants without prior pituitary surgery and the overall population, including age and mean IGF-1 level, except for time since  acromegaly  diagnosis  ( median of 139 months for  overall  and 177 months for  participants without prior pituitary surgery).

Seven of the 8 participants ( 87.5% ) without prior pituitary surgery had microadenomas at the time of diagnosis. Tumour size at the time of diagnosis was missing for 1 participant.

Similar to the overall population, a higher percentage of participants were receiving octreotide compared with  lanreotide  (6/8  [75.0%]  versus  2/8  [25.0%])  as  their  long-acting  SRL  therapy  prior  to  study enrolment, with all participants receiving mid (n=3) and high doses (n=5) of their prior SRLs.

In the RC phase, of the 4 participants without prior pituitary surgery in the paltusotine group, the final paltusotine dose was 40 mg QD for 2 participants and 60 mg QD for the other 2 participants.

During the OLE phase, the final dose of paltusotine was 40 mg QD for 5 participants (2 paltusotine, 3 placebo), and 60 mg QD for 3 participants (2 paltusotine, 1 placebo) at the time of the data cut-off.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

In the paltusotine group, mean IGF-1 levels &lt;1.0×ULN were maintained at all scheduled visits from Baseline through Week 36, whereas in the placebo group, mean IGF-1 levels were increased at Week 8, by which time 1 of the 4 participants in the placebo group had received rescue therapy.

Participants in the paltusotine group continued to maintain IGF-1 levels &lt;1.0×ULN up through Week 96 in the OLE phase, although the numbers were small.

Participants in the placebo group showed a rapid decrease over the first two weeks in the OLE Phase that was maintained to Week 96.

Overall, all 8 participants without prior surgery had IGF-1 levels &lt;1.0×ULN at their last available IGF-1  assessment  in  the  OLE  Phase.  These  results  showing  consistent  and  durable  maintenance  of response to paltusotine are consistent with the overall population for the study.

In the RC phase, these 8 participants had stable pituitary tumour volume (evaluated by MRI by central reviewer)  at  EOR.  In  the  OLE  phase,  pituitary  tumour  volume  assessments  were  available  for  3 participants, and all were stable.

## 5.3.3. Clinical studies in special populations

The efficacy of paltusotine was evaluated for selected subpopulations for each of the phase 3 studies.

No notable subgroup effects were observed for any subgroup in either study CRN00808-08 or study CRN00808-09 , showing that the paltusotine treatment effect was consistent across subgroups.

For both studies, the subgroups of unknown ethnicity and non-White and unknown race, the 95% CI for the treatment difference included zero. However, the sample size for these subgroups was small, and the treatment differences were greater than zero and directionally favoured Paltusotine.

## By region

In the two pivotal registration trials (study CRN00808-09 and study CRN00808-08 ), a total of 169 patients were treated. Of these, a total of 48 patients ( 28.4% ) were from Europe.

For study CRN00808-08, 8.1% of participants were from the US and 64.0% from Rest of World (RoW). In study CRN00808-09, 19.0% were from the US and 51.7% from RoW.

Clinically relevant decreases in IGF-1 were observed in all regional subgroups. Likewise, reduction in GH levels  and  proportion  of  participants  who  experienced  clinically  meaningful  change  was  observed consistently across subgroups.  Subgroup analyses did not reveal any significant differences in efficacy across regions.

## By IGF-1 Baseline Levels, Age, Gender, and BMI

Reduction in IGF-1 from baseline was generally comparable, irrespectively of baseline IGF-1 (median &lt;1.78 or ≥ 1.68 ng/mL or median&lt; 0.86 ng/mL or ≥ 0.86 ng/mL).

Although the number of participants age ≥ 65 years was low, the observed treatment effects and disease control was similar to that observed in participants &lt;65 years of age. Similarly, gender did not impact the observed treatment effect, with males and females experiencing similar effects.

BMI did not have any significant effect on the IGF-1 response to paltusotine, with participants with BMI ≥ 30 kg/m 2  experiencing comparable clinical effects as participants with BMI &lt;30 kg/m 2 . The observed treatment effect of paltusotine was comparable.

<div style=\"page-break-after: always\"></div>

## Participants Without Prior Pituitary Surgery

First-line  therapy  for  acromegaly  is  pituitary  tumour  resection,  typically  by  transsphenoidal  surgery. However, for some patients, surgical removal is not an option, or they choose not to have surgery or may experience delays before a surgical  appointment  becomes  available.  For  many  others,  surgical intervention  does  not  achieve  IGF-1  normalization  and  adequately  resolve  acromegaly  symptoms, necessitating additional adjunctive pharmacotherapy.

In the paltusotine clinical studies, the majority of participants enrolled had undergone prior surgical intervention for their pituitary tumours.

However, a small subset of participants ( N=20 ) across the acromegaly studies (6 participants in studies CRN00808-08 ,  8  participants in study CRN00808-09 ,  and 6 participants in study CRN00808-05 ) were surgically naïve but had received pharmacological intervention prior to study enrolment.

A post-hoc analysis was performed to evaluate the data for participants without prior surgery (see more details in the section Ancillary analyses ).

Although the small number of participants in this post-hoc analysis limits its use, the overall results demonstrate  a  consistent  paltusotine  treatment  effect  across  a  broad  spectrum  of  acromegaly participants. In participants without prior pituitary surgery who received pharmacological therapy prior to enrolment in the phase 3 studies, paltusotine demonstrated a rapid, consistent, and durable treatment effect, as well as stable pituitary volume, consistent with the efficacy results with paltusotine in the overall population of the phase 3 studies.

## 5.3.4. Supportive studies

3 supportive phase 2 studies were provided: study CRN00808-03 , CRN00808-02 and CRN0080805 . All the details are in Dose-response studies of this report.

## 5.3.5. Analysis performed across trials (pooled analyses and metaanalysis)

## Primary Efficacy Endpoint

The primary efficacy endpoint for both studies defined as IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN based on the average of the last 2 measurements collected at Week 22 and Week 24 (EOR) in Study CRN00808-08 and at Week 34 and Week 36 (EOR) in study CRN00808-09 . Both studies met statistical significance on the primary endpoint (p&lt;0.0001) favouring paltusotine over placebo.

In study CRN00808-08 , the proportion of participants who achieved IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN at EOR was 55.6% (30 of 54) in the paltusotine group compared with 5.3% (3 of 57) in the placebo group.

Study CRN00808-09 the proportion of participants who maintained biochemical response (IGF-1 level ≤ 1.0 × ULN) at EOR in the paltusotine group was 83.3% (25 of 30) in the paltusotine group compared with 3.6% (1 of 28) in the placebo group.

Results of prespecified sensitivity analyses to assess the robustness of the primary efficacy analyses for study CRN00808-08 and study CRN008008-09 were consistent with the primary efficacy endpoint results.

## Secondary Efficacy Endpoints

Similar secondary endpoints are summarized together for both phase 3 studies. Study CRN00808-08 has an additional secondary endpoint of the proportion of participants who achieved IGF-1 &lt;1.3×ULN at EOR.

assessment  prior  to  rescue.  Both  studies  met  statistical  significance  on  the  secondary  endpoint (p&lt;0.0001) favouring paltusotine over placebo.

## 3) Change from Baseline to EOR in Total ASD Score

Table 57 presents results if no rescue therapy was administered or was based on the last available assessment prior to rescue therapy. Both studies met statistical significance on the secondary endpoint favouring paltusotine over placebo.

In Study CNR00808-08 , the LS mean (±SE) changes from Baseline to EOT in total ASD score were 2.669 (±1.4221) for the paltusotine group and 2.754 (±1.3641) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -5.423 (95% CI: -9.070, -1.776) ( p=0.0039 ).

In Study CRN00808-09 , the LS means (±SE) for the change from Baseline to EOT in total ASD score were -0.606 (±1.5044) for the paltusotine group and 4.558 (±1.5926) for the placebo group, with a treatment difference of -5.164 (95% CI: -9.536, -0.792) ( p=0.0216 ).
